She grips her massive carpet bag of a purse and places it beside her on the seat, silently telling me I can’t sit with her. I wouldn’t want to anyway. I hate riding the bus, but I wish it ran later at night so I had a reliable way to get home after work. The heavy scent of exhaust creeps into my nose and intensifies my growing headache. My workday is just starting, and I already have visions of crawling into bed and returning to sleep.
